title: Method and system for auto-detecting induction motor fault
abstract: The present invention relates to a method for auto-detecting an induction motor fault comprising measuring the current supplied to the induction motor to obtain a current signal data in a time domain, converting the current signal data in the time domain into a current signal data in a frequency domain, determining a fault frequency amplitude from a calculation using a rotor speed at a rotor frequency where the amplitude is maximum in a range between induction motor's synchronous frequency and induction motor's full load driving frequency, determining a comparative fault frequency amplitude by determining the highest amplitude in a predetermined frequency range, and analyzing the induction motor fault by performing according to the specified conditions according to the present invention. The method may further comprise predicting an induction motor's remaining lifespan.
claims:
Claims WHAT IS CLAIMED IS: A method for auto -detecting an induction motor fault comprising: measuring the current supplied to the induction motor to obtain a current signal data in a time domain, converting the current signal data in the time domain into a current signal data in a frequency domain, determining a fault frequency amplitude obtained from a calculation using a rotor speed at a rotor frequency where the amplitude is maximum in a range between induction motorâs synchronous frequency and induction motorâs full load driving frequency, determining a comparative fault frequency amplitude by determining the highest amplitude in a predetermined frequency range, and analyzing the induction motor fault by performing according to the following conditions: in a case where the highest comparative fault frequency amplitude is in the predetermined frequency range, the higher amplitude between the fault frequency amplitude obtained from the calculation and the comparative fault frequency amplitude is selected for a comparison with predetermined fault criteria, or in a case where the highest comparative fault frequency amplitude is outside the predetermined frequency range, the fault frequency amplitude obtained from the calculation is selected for a comparison with the predetermined fault criteria. The method according to claim 1, wherein the induction motor fault is caused by a rotor damage, and the fault frequency amplitude obtained from the calculation is an amplitude of a fault frequency caused by the rotor damage which is determined by an equation below: f rc = (lÂ±2ks)fi where frc is the fault frequency caused by the rotor damage, fi is a power supply frequency, k is a constant which is a positive integer, 

 s is a slip value which is determined by an equation s = (n s -n r )/n s where n s is an induction motorâs synchronous speed, n r is a rotor speed at a rotor frequency where the amplitude is maximum in a range between the induction motorâs synchronous frequency and the induction motorâs full load driving frequency. The method according to claim 1, wherein the induction motor fault is caused by an air gap imbalance, and the fault frequency amplitude obtained from the calculation is an amplitude of a fault frequency caused by the air gap imbalance which is determined by an equation below: fee = fi[Rs((l-s)/p))Â± r|ws] Â± fi((l-s)/p) where fee is the fault frequency causesd by the air gap imbalance, fi is the power supply frequency, R s is a number of rotor slot, s is the slip value which is determined by the equation s = (n s -n r )/n s where n s is the induction motorâs synchronous speed, n r is the rotor speed at the rotor frequency where the amplitude is maximum in a range between the induction motorâs synchronous frequency and the induction motorâs full load driving frequency, p is a number of pair of magnetic poles, r|ws is a constant which is an odd positive interger. The method according to claim 1, wherein the induction motor fault is caused by a stator damage, and the fault frequency amplitude obtained from the calculation is an amplitude of a fault frequency caused by the stator damage which is determined by an equation below: fst = fi[(n/p(l-s))Â±k] where fst is the fault frequency caused by the stator damage, 

 17 fi is the power supply frequency, n is a constant which is a positive integer, p is the number of pair of magetic poles, s is the slip value which is determined by the equation s = (n s -n r )/n s where n s is the induction motorâs synchronous speed, n r is the rotor speed at the rotor frequency where the amplitude is maximum in a range between the induction motorâs synchronous frequency and the induction motorâs full load driving frequency, k is the constant which is a positive integer. The method according to claim 1 or 2, wherein the induction motor fault is caused by the rotor damage and the predetermined frequency range is defined by determining the amplitude at the power supply frequency, defining a start point of the frequency range, which is the point where the amplitude slope is lower than specified values both on the left side and the right side of the power supply frequency, and defining an end point of the frequency range, which is the point where the frequency is 3 Hz away from the start point towards the side which is away from the power supply frequency. The method according to claim 1 or 3, wherein the induction motor fault is caused by the air gap imbalance, and the predetermined frequency range is defined by using the fault frequency caused by the air gap imbalance as a reference point and defining the frequency range towards the left side and the right side to be away from the reference point with a distance of 5% of a slot passing frequency, wherein the slot passing frequency is determined by an equation below: frs = fi[Rs((l-s)/p)Â±r|ws] where frs is the slot passing frequency, fi is the power supply frequency, R s is the number of rotor slot, s is the slip value which is determined by the equation 

 18 s = (n s -n r )/n s where n s is the induction motorâs synchronous speed, n r is the rotor speed at the rotor frequency where the amplitude is maximum in a range between the induction motorâs synchronous frequency and the induction motorâs full load driving frequency, p is the number of pair of magetic poles, r|ws is the constant which is an odd positive interger. The method according to claim 1 or 4, wherein the induction motor fault is caued by the stator damage, and the predetermined frequency range is defined by using the fault frequency caused by the stator damage as a reference point and defining the frequency range towards the left side and the right side to be away from the reference point with a distance of 5% of power supply frequency. The method according to claim 7 further comprising determining whether a current unbalance is in an acceptable range prior to defining the predetermined frequency range. The method according to claim 8, wherein an acceptable current unbalance does not exceed 5%. The method according to claim 1, wherein converting the current signal data in the time domain into the current signal data in the frequency domain is performed using a fast Fourier transform (FFT) method. The method according to claim 1 further comprising demodulating the current signal data in the frequency domain to eliminate an electrical signal and determine a mechanical frequency signal. The method according to claim 1 further comprising predicting an induction motorâs remaining lifespan which is performed by creating a trend line of the fault frequency amplitude, which is obtained by selecting the higher amplitude between the fault frequency amplitude obtained from the calculation and the comparative fault frequency amplitude, and determining the induction motorâs remaining lifespan using a regression analysis method to analyze the trend line of the fault frequency amplitude, which is obtained by selecting the higher amplitude between the fault frequency amplitude obtained from the calculation and the comparative fault frequency amplitude. 

 19 A system for auto -detecting an induction motor fault comprising: a current measuring device for measuring the current supplied to the induction motor to obtain a current signal data in a time domain, a processor which is provided for converting the current signal data in the time domain into a current signal data in a frequency domain, determining a fault frequency amplitude obtained from a calculation using a rotor speed at a rotor frequency where the amplitude is maximum in a range between induction motorâs synchronous frequency and induction motorâs full load driving frequency, determining a comparative fault frequency amplitude by determining the highest amplitude in a predetermined frequency range, and analyzing the induction motor fault by performing according to the following conditions: in a case where the highest comparative fault frequency amplitude is in the predetermined frequency range, the higher amplitude between the fault frequency amplitude obtained from the calculation and the comparative fault frequency amplitude is selected for a comparison with predetermined fault criteria, or in a case where the highest comparative fault frequency amplitude is outside the predetermined frequency range, the fault frequency amplitude obtained from the calculation is selected for a comparison with the predetermined fault criteria, and a display device for displaying the data obtained from the processor. The system according to claim 19, wherein the processor is also provided to determine whether a current unbalance is in an acceptable range prior to defining the predetermined frequency range.  23 determining the induction motorâs remaining lifespan using a regression analysis method to analyze the trend line of the fault frequency amplitude, which is obtained by selecting the higher amplitude between the fault frequency amplitude obtained from the calculation and the comparative fault frequency amplitude. 25. The system according to claim 13 or 25, wherein the display device displays the data comprising the induction motorâs fault status, the induction motorâs remaining lifespan, the current unbalance, the trend line of the fault frequency amplitude, the trend line of the rotor speed, and the current.
